title: Classic Outlook Receives Major Update: Microsoft Announces AI-Driven Email Drafting Feature to Be Introduced This Year
excerpt: Microsoft is actively promoting the intelligent upgrade of its office software. According to the latest plan, Microsoft intends to push the Copilot feature upda
content_html:
Microsoft is actively promoting the intelligent upgrade of its office software. According to the latest plan, Microsoft intends to push the Copilot feature update to classic Outlook users on Windows 10 and Windows 11 platforms by the end of the year.

AI Empowers Email Writing

The most core highlight of this update is the addition of an AI draft email function. Users with the relevant license will be able to activate the AI directly in the compose window with one click, allowing the system to assist in writing a new email or polishing an existing draft.

Microsoft's move aims to let old users also enjoy the convenient experience brought by intelligence. However, some analysts point out that this change may break the habits of some traditional users, even increasing the complexity of daily use.

Driving Software Upgrade and Iteration

In fact, Microsoft has always been guiding users to migrate to the new version of Outlook. The team is continuously enriching the functional ecosystem of the new application, trying to attract more users to actively complete the version transition.

Although the development focus has long shifted to the new version, Microsoft still chose to add AI features to the classic version. Whether this strategy can balance the habits of old users and the trend of intelligence remains to be tested by the market later.

content_html_zh:
微软正在积极推动办公软件的智能化升级。 根据最新计划，微软拟在年底前向 Windows 10 和 Windows 11 平台上的经典 Outlook 用户推送 Copilot 功能更新。

人工智能赋能电子邮件写作

本次更新最核心的亮点就是新增了AI草稿邮件功能。 拥有相关许可证的用户将能够直接在撰写窗口中一键激活人工智能，让系统协助撰写新电子邮件或润色现有草稿。

微软此举旨在让老用户也享受到智能带来的便捷体验。 不过，有分析指出，这一改变可能会打破部分传统用户的习惯，甚至增加日常使用的复杂度。

驱动软件升级迭代

事实上，微软一直在引导用户迁移到新版本的Outlook。 团队正在不断丰富新应用的功能生态，试图吸引更多用户积极完成版本过渡。

尽管开发重点早已转移到新版本，但微软仍然选择在经典版本中加入AI功能。 这一策略能否平衡老用户的使用习惯和智能化趋势，还有待市场后期的检验。
